title: Creamy Irish Potato Casserole
description: A hearty, garlicky Irish dish featuring thyme and puff pastry, perfect for a lazy Sunday. This low-glycemic-friendly casserole is sure to become a family favorite.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, combine the sliced potatoes and melted butter, tossing to coat.
In a small bowl, mix together the minced garlic and chopped thyme.
Add the garlic and thyme mixture to the potatoes and toss to combine.
Roll out the puff pastry on a lightly floured surface to a large rectangle.
In a large skillet, cook the potato mixture over medium heat until the potatoes are slightly tender.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the heavy cream, grated cheddar cheese, salt, and black pepper.
Pour the cream mixture over the potato mixture in the skillet and stir to combine.
Spoon the potato and cream mixture onto one half of the puff pastry, leaving a 1/2 inch border around the edges.
Brush the edges of the pastry with the beaten egg and fold the other half of the pastry over the filling.
Press the edges of the pastry to seal and cut a few slits in the top to allow steam to escape.
